definition: The US dollar is in a capex-sustained plateau with an AI capital flow premium estimated at 5-8 cents EUR/USD (wide confidence intervals: 3-12 cents). DXY at 103-107 despite 100-150bp Fed cuts confirms rate differentials are not the primary driver. TIC data shows $40-60B/month net equity inflows concentrated in tech.
evidence: TIC data: $40-60B/month net equity inflows concentrated in tech
ECB-Fed differential narrowed ~100bp+ with minimal EUR/USD response
Historical parallel: 1997-2001 DXY +25% without systematic rate advantage
AI premium is structural (3-5 year), not cyclical
open_questions: The 5-8 cent premium estimate could range from 3-12 cents
Whether capital flow reversal would be gradual or discontinuous
